x-schema-changelog: 1.3.0 (issue #296 / fw-adr-0021 §1): added optional token_budget and jit_file_list to bounded_codex_exception as leaf-task context-assembly inputs for dispatch_scope: single_task. Both fields are optional in the schema; the context-assembly rule and tech-lead manual guide population (gate/manual enforces, schema stays lax on these). | 1.2.0 (issue #293 / fw-adr-0021): added delegated_role, task_ref, dispatch_scope to bounded_codex_exception; when codex_permission_flag is true, delegated_role and dispatch_scope are now required alongside permitted_role_owned_action (task_ref is gate-enforced only via check_delegated_specialist in validate-handoff.py — intentionally absent from then.required so the schema stays lax on task_ref while the gate is strict). token_budget_hint is out of scope for this change (separate fw-adr-0021 concern). | 1.1.0 (fw-adr-0023 D2 Option S): removed 'activity' from required[]; property retained as optional/deprecated for backward-compat with handoff files written before the sidecar migration. Runtime telemetry now written to docs/handoffs/<task_id>.activity.jsonl.

evidence_kind.description: Classifies whether this evidence is independently accepted (from a non-owner role satisfying the gate, e.g. code-reviewer artifact, security-engineer sign-off, hook-captured test rerun, researcher-stewarded customer-truth record) or a worker_report (the implementing worker's own claim). Worker self-attestation cannot satisfy final test, review, security, or customer-truth gates; the gate enforces this via actor_role checks.

source.description: Origin of the evidence, e.g. 'PostToolUse', 'CUSTOMER_NOTES.md', 'ad-hoc-chat', or a reviewer artifact path.

artifact.description: Path or identifier for the evidence artifact, e.g. a review document or security sign-off record.

name.description: Name or identifier for this evidence entry, typically the test suite or review label.

type.description: Informal evidence category label within a gate list, e.g. 'test', 'review'.

execution_mode.description: Execution mode identifier for this bounded-Codex session (e.g. 'bounded-codex').

permitted_role_owned_action.description: The specific role-owned action this bounded-Codex exception is scoped to. Required when codex_permission_flag is true; cannot be absent or empty when permission is granted, ensuring role ownership cannot be waived.

codex_permission_flag.description: Explicit permission grant for bounded Codex on this handoff. Must be true for bounded-Codex execution to be allowed; false is an explicit denial. Absence of the entire bounded_codex_exception object is also a denial. This field must not be inferred — the gate checks it directly.

allowed_paths.description: Paths bounded Codex may write within this exception. Bounded Codex receives no broader path authority than the active handoff declares.

forbidden_paths.description: Paths explicitly forbidden for bounded Codex even if covered by allowed_paths. Forbidden paths override allowed paths.

required_evidence.description: Evidence gate identifiers that must be satisfied before this bounded-Codex exception is accepted as complete.

expiry_or_completion_condition.description: Condition under which this bounded-Codex exception expires or is considered complete.

delegated_role.description: Canonical role name the delegated session must adopt (e.g. 'software-engineer'). Required when codex_permission_flag is true (fw-adr-0021 §3). Must not be 'tech-lead' — delegated mode cannot adopt the orchestrator role; the gate rejects this and the agent must halt if it encounters it.

task_ref.description: Reference to the single leaf task this delegated session is scoped to (e.g. 'T041' pointing into tasks.md). Signals single-task dispatch; the delegated agent executes only this task.

dispatch_scope.description: Scope of this dispatch. 'single_task' is required for bounded-Codex leaf-task dispatch (fw-adr-0021). 'feature' is the default for orchestrator-level planning handoffs and is never a valid input for a delegated-specialist or bounded-Codex invocation.

token_budget.description: Leaf-task token-budget band from the T### task entry in tasks.md (docs/templates/task-template.md § 'Token budget'). Guides the delegated session's context loading: tiny/small sessions load minimal files; xl signals a complex task that may require splitting. Optional — feature-altitude handoffs omit this field. Populate for dispatch_scope: single_task dispatches (issue #296 / fw-adr-0021 §1).

jit_file_list.description: Just-in-time file list for the leaf task: the paths the delegated session should Read first, copied from the T### task entry's JIT file list field (docs/templates/task-template.md § 'Token budget'). Keeps context loading minimal and targeted. Optional — feature-altitude handoffs omit this field. Populate for dispatch_scope: single_task dispatches (issue #296 / fw-adr-0021 §1).

external_tool_activity_ref.description: INPUT-ONLY reference to external-tool activity (e.g. llmdc, Speckit). Must never appear inside verification.* or completion.evidence; no field here is read by evidence helpers as accepted evidence. Note: this structural separation is enforced by the evidence helpers (which only read verification.*/completion.evidence), not by JSON Schema validation itself.

tool_type.description: Which external tool produced this activity reference.

activity_type.description: The category of activity from the external tool (e.g. 'planning', 'documentation', 'task-scaffold', 'checklist').

affected_artifact.description: Path or identifier of the handoff or artifact this activity relates to.

canonical_role_owner.description: The canonical role that owns and must accept or reject this activity's output.

cited_active_handoff.description: Task ID or path of the active handoff this external-tool activity is associated with.

limitations.description: Notes on what this external-tool activity cannot do (e.g. cannot approve gates, cannot record customer truth).

speckit_feature_path.description: Speckit-only: feature path within the Speckit artifact.

speckit_source_authority.description: Speckit-only: source authority classification. Always 'input-only' or 'pending-role-acceptance'; never accepted evidence.

github_issue.description: Optional bidirectional link to the coordination GitHub issue for this handoff (feature 014, FR-017). Value is an issue number or URL. Absence keeps the handoff valid for single-operator and offline projects.

external_tool_activity.description: Optional INPUT-ONLY list of external-tool activity references (llmdc, Speckit). These are informational inputs to canonical-role work; they are structurally separate from verification.* and completion.evidence and cannot satisfy any evidence gate.

activity.description: DEPRECATED (fw-adr-0023 D2 Option S, schema v1.1.0). Runtime telemetry moved to docs/handoffs/<task_id>.activity.jsonl sidecar. This property is retained as optional for backward-compatibility with handoff files written before the migration. New handoff files should omit it.
